last_context: 2026-06-25: 7-item vision update complete. 1) API History Viewer — per-chat raw JSON log opened in file viewer tab. 2) Generic File Viewer Tabs — read-only tabs alongside chats. 3) Parallel Tool Execution — independent tools run in parallel. 4) Rich UsageRecord — cache tokens, latency, tier, error info, raw JSON path. 5) Text Completion mode for Text Actions — chatMode field on TextAction. 6) App Data Locations — System Info category in Settings. 7) 14-tool provider-agnostic surface — text_editor replaced by read_file/apply_diff/write_to_file/list_files/search_files. Tool count 10→14. Per-chat workspace isolation. Out-of-workspace approval model.

flagged_concerns: Auto-Summarize context overflow requires separate API call costing tokens
Text completion APIs being deprecated by some providers
WebView2 runtime dependency adds ~100MB to install size
bash tool security — workspace isolation is app-enforced, not OS-level sandbox
WebSocket server security model needs review
Global keyboard hooks may trigger antivirus false positives
VACUUM requires temporary free disk space equal to database size
Rich text bidirectional text rendering complexity
Google Cloud Storage dependency for backups
Locked chat encryption — permanent lockout risk must be communicated
Git wiki version control — GitHub token storage requires DPAPI encryption
Model comparison — per-panel multi-turn conversations = N independent API streams
Skill dependency management — 11 skills require Python/Node.js/LibreOffice none bundled
Workspace isolation is app-enforced not OS sandbox
present_files auto-copy from workspace may cause UI pauses for large files
Memory tool separate from wiki — two knowledge persistence mechanisms risk of user confusion
14-tool provider-agnostic schemas — model compliance varies, must be tested per provider
Out-of-workspace file read approval — Auto-Approve removes safety gate, Ask causes frequent dialogs
Per-chat workspace isolation — app-scoped, not OS-sandboxed
Raw API JSON log per chat may grow very large for long conversations
Parallel tool execution — model-dependent, some models may not batch independent calls
Generic file viewer Markdown rendering — no wiki features, risk of user confusion
